danger_signals.visual_form: Colored rocks cascade from top of opponent's play field
danger_signals.meaning: Opponent has successfully chained block eliminations; incoming threat that reduces play space

danger_signals.visual_form: Blobs or rocks stacking upward toward middle columns
danger_signals.meaning: Game over condition imminent; loss state approaching

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy character portrait or visual representation during match setup
danger_signals.meaning: Challenge escalating; boss or stronger opponent engagement

safety_signals.signal: Empty play space with room to maneuver
safety_signals.visual_form: Lower density of blobs on screen; visible blank grid areas
safety_signals.meaning: Player has control; time to plan moves and set up chains

safety_signals.visual_form: Cascading blob eliminations with visual effects; multiple groups disappearing in sequence
safety_signals.meaning: Player executing offensive strategy; safe position while rocks transfer to opponent

safety_signals.visual_form: Kirby shown conversing or in confident posture during cutscenes
safety_signals.meaning: Narrative progression; player advancing through tournament successfully

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Kirby character and allied Dream Land inhabitants
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Whispy Woods, Kracko, Meta Knight rendered as tournament opponents with distinct visual designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fountain of Dreams location; forest setting for preliminary matches

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: King Dedede as tournament host and final boss opponent
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: King Dedede visually distinct from other opponents; positioned as ultimate challenge
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fountain of Dreams as final battle location; tournament framing establishes hierarchical conflict

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Forest setting; weaker opponents like Whispy Woods introduced early
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kirby matches against progressively stronger foes; dialogue reflects competitive buildup

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Opponents escalate in visual complexity and recognition (Kracko, Meta Knight)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Difficulty increases; player faces opponents with established threat patterns from prior Kirby games

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fountain of Dreams location; King Dedede cinematic appearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final confrontation; Kirby's dialogue becomes more sarcastic and confrontational per Western version characterization

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tournament bracket implied through sequential opponent appearances
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cinematic trash-talking between rounds; Kirby personality shifts from typical friendly demeanor to competitive aggression

environmental_storytelling.technique: Opponent selection order communicates narrative hierarchy
environmental_storytelling.example: Forest enemies (Whispy Woods) appear early; King Dedede appears at Fountain of Dreams location, signaling ultimate challenge and world-state significance
Opponents are pulled from prior Kirby games (Dream Land, Adventure series), establishing lore continuity; their visual designs remain recognizable
Fountain of Dreams serves as final battle venue, a significant location in Kirby lore, elevating the narrative weight of the King Dedede confrontation
Western version includes full-sentence trash-talking conversations between Kirby and opponents; sarcastic tone indicates competitive escalation and Kirby's personality shift during tournament stress

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Personality shifts from typical friendly demeanor to sarcastic and confrontational; dialogues between rounds show competitive aggression unique to this game

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Opponent characters (Whispy Woods, Kracko, Meta Knight, King Dedede)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Engage in trash-talking with Kirby; visual presence in cinematics between rounds signals challenge intensity
Cascade onto opponent's screen; quantity and intensity correlate with player's chain reaction success, creating visible pressure on opponent
Visual rise toward middle columns top row signals imminent loss; stack density increases visual stress to communicate danger

notes: Kirby's Avalanche communicates world-state primarily through tournament narrative framing and opponent progression rather than environmental hazards. Danger is signaled through game mechanics (incoming rocks, rising stacks) rather than diegetic environmental cues. The Western version's emphasis on Kirby's sarcastic personality in cutscenes serves as behavioral world-state indicator—Kirby's characterization shifts under tournament pressure, differentiating this title from other Kirby games. Faction indicators are minimal; the game frames conflict as tournament competition rather than ideological opposition. Visual storytelling relies on opponent recognition from series history and cinematic dialogue tone.
